news.summary: The US Department of Justice filed a civil forfeiture complaint to seize over $24 million in cryptocurrency from Rustam Rafailevich Gallyamov, a Russian national accused of developing the Qakbot malware. Gallyamov, a 48-year-old from Moscow, is allegedly the malware developer behind the Qakbot botnet. The DOJ announced the charges against him, emphasizing their determination to hold cybercriminals accountable and use legal tools to identify, charge, and disrupt criminal activity. The indictment states that Gallyamov operated Qakbot as early as 2008, infecting thousands of computers to establish a botnet, which was later used by others to deploy ransomware like Prolock, Dopplepaymer, Egregor, REvil, Conti, and others. Despite a 2023 operation that disrupted the Qakbot botnet and seized over 170 Bitcoin, $4 million in USDt, and USDC stablecoins from him, Gallyamov allegedly continued his criminal activities. This ongoing effort aims to identify, disrupt, and compensate victims of cybercrime, with the FBI's Los Angeles Field Office noting that Gallyamov persisted in deploying alternative methods to distribute his malware even after the initial operation.
news.headline: DOJ seizes $24M in crypto from Qakbot malware dev; Russian national accused, indictment filed; DOJ determined to hold cybercriminals accountable.

news.summary: Ripple's cryptocurrency (XRP) surged past $2.45, reclaiming a significant psychological level, driven by factors like the launch of a U.S.-listed XRP futures ETF, a new coin burn mechanism, and positive technical indicators. The price climb started from $2.32 and witnessed a rising open interest. Additionally, the launch of a euro-denominated stablecoin on the XRP Ledger under the EU's regulatory framework added to the positive sentiment. Noteworthy developments include the introduction of the first futures-based XRP ETF by Volatility Shares on NASDAQ, with 80% asset allocation to XRP futures contracts for institutional investors seeking regulated exposure. The article also mentions trading opportunities on Plus500 and Coinbase for new traders, with rewards for completing educational courses and making qualifying trades. This article and further details are available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Ripple (XRP) rises above $2.45 following news of XRP futures ETF launch, coin burn, and euro stablecoin addition.

news.summary: Dogecoin (DOGE) saw a 3.11% rise in the past 24 hours, reaching $0.25, and a 7.94% increase over the week. A $40 million sell-off by major whales, the Dogecoin and Solana Bridge launch, and the SEC's Dogecoin ETF approval delay influenced this. Blockchain developer, Psy, introduced a bridge merging Solana Network with Dogecoin. (NASDAQ: WDAY) shares dropped by more than 11% after releasing first-quarter results, reporting earnings of $2.23 per share, surpassing the analyst consensus estimate of $2.01 by 10.95%. news.summary: On May 12, 2025, hackers hijacked Curve Finance's ".fi" domain through DNS manipulation and led users to a fake website, attempting to drain their wallets. This was the second attack in a week and similar to a previous one in August 2022. The attack targeted the DNS layer, not the protocol's smart contracts. Different DNS hijacking methods were explained, such as local DNS hijack and router hijack. The attackers altered DNS records with access to the registrar "iwantmyname." The backend remained unaffected, but millions were lost through a spoofed front end. Curve Finance responded by redirecting to a temporary domain, securing fund management, and initiating mitigation efforts. The hack raised concerns about the vulnerability of decentralized finance (DeFi) interfaces to DNS hijacking and highlighted the importance of bridging the gap between decentralized protocols and centralized interfaces for better security. To prevent such attacks, crypto projects can integrate decentralized DNS alternatives, use decentralized file storage systems, implement DNSSEC, secure registrar accounts, and educate users. It is crucial for the crypto industry to explore decentralized web infrastructure like IPFS and ENS for enhanced security.
news.headline: DNS hijacking occurs when hackers alter DNS records to redirect users to fake sites. Curve Finance suffered this on May 12, 2025, as attackers rerouted ".fi" domain, attempting to drain wallets. This was the second attack in a week, where users were misled to provide wallet signatures. The hack didn't breach protocols but targeted the DNS layer. DNS is vital for the internet, functioning like a phonebook. This isn't the first time Curve Finance faced such an attack. Attackers can conduct DNS hijacking through various methods like local DNS hijack, router hijack, or a man-in-the-middle attack. To deal with DNS hijacking, projects can integrate decentralized DNS alternatives, use decentralized file storage systems, implement DNSSEC, secure registrar accounts, and educate users.

news.summary: Avalanche (CRYPTO: AVAX) experienced a significant rally, surging 8.5% from trading in the low $20s to reach $25.1. Prior to this increase, the token had been stabilizing around $22 and has now reached close to $25.6. This surge follows the partnership announcement with the International Federation of Association Football (FIFA) to launch their Layer-1 blockchain on Avalanche's network. Avalanche recently introduced the Avalanche9000 upgrade, reducing costs by approximately 90%. The collaboration with FIFA is deemed as a crucial development in the blockchain infrastructure evolution by Avalanche, showcasing the network's reliability. The prominent partnership with FIFA highlights Avalanche's attractiveness in the realm of sports and enterprise blockchains. news.headline: Avalanche's price surges 8.5% to $25.1 after deal with FIFA; recent upgrade reduces costs by 90%, now at $25.6.

news.summary: A recent report revealed that the unemployment rate in the United States dropped to 4.6% in the month of September, marking a significant decrease from the previous month's rate of 5.2%. The economy also added 450,000 jobs during this period, exceeding expectations and indicating a positive trend in the labor market. Additionally, the report highlighted a notable increase in average hourly wages by 0.6%. These figures suggest a strengthening economic recovery and provide optimism for the future job market outlook.

news.summary: Ethereum (ETH) is currently priced at $2,654.33, marking a 0.77% increase in the last 24 hours. Throughout the week, the token saw a 1.95% growth, and a substantial 48.44% surge over the month. In a notable milestone, Ethereum surpassed Bank of America Corp (BAC) in market cap, with ETH's market cap at $327.82 billion overtaking BAC's $326.20 billion. This achievement highlights Ethereum's rapid growth compared to Bank of America, a company with a history spanning over a century. This development instills confidence in investors and demonstrates the increasing dominance of crypto over traditional finance. This shift suggests a changing landscape beyond just technological aspects.
news.headline: Ethereum's price rises 48%, surpasses Bank of America with market cap of $327.82B. Stripe integrates stablecoins.

news.summary: In a recent update, it has been reported that there has been a significant rise in the number of COVID-19 cases in the city. The total number of confirmed cases has now reached over 10,000, with a daily increase of 500 new cases. The situation is becoming more concerning as hospitals are reaching full capacity, with 80% of ICU beds currently occupied. Additionally, the death toll has climbed to over 200. Officials are urging the public to adhere to safety guidelines and protocols to help curb the spread of the virus.

news.summary: Shares of Buckle, Inc. (NYSE: BKE) are trading down in premarket on Friday following the release of their first-quarter results. The company reported earnings per share of 70 cents, surpassing the analyst consensus of 69 cents. Their quarterly sales amounted to $272.12 million, marking a 3.7% increase compared to the previous year and surpassing the Street's expectation of $268.06 million. Comparable store net sales for the quarter ending on May 3 saw a 3% year-over-year increase. Online sales also saw growth, rising by 4.5% to $46.4 million.
news.headline: Buckle Inc. (NYSE: BKE) stock down in premarket after reporting Q1 earnings; EPS at 70 cents, sales at $272.12 million, online sales up 4.5%.

news.summary: Frontline Plc's (NYSE: FRO) stock saw an uptick in trading today. The company's reported revenue stood at $427.9 million, exceeding the consensus estimate of $264.2 million. However, the adjusted EPS dropped to 18 cents from 62 cents compared to the previous year, falling short of the projected 23 cents. Notably, the spot time charter equivalent earnings (TCEs) for different tanker types were reported as follows: $37,200 for VLCCs (compared to $48,100 last year), $31,200 for Suezmax tankers (versus $45,800 last year), and $22,300 for LR2/Aframax tankers (as opposed to $54,300 the prior year) per day. By the end of the first quarter of 2025, Frontline had 887 contracted ballast days for VLCCs, 306 for Suezmax tankers, and 216 for LR2/Aframax tankers. The company's fleet as of March 31, 2025, comprised 81 vessels, including 41 VLCCs, 22 Suezmax tankers, and 18 LR2/Aframax tankers, with a total capacity of approximately... (Further details can be found on Benzinga.com)
news.headline: Frontline Plc reports revenue of $427.9 million, exceeding consensus of $264.2 million; EPS declines to 18 cents. Spot TCEs for VLCCs at $37,200, Suezmax tankers at $31,200, and LR2/Aframax tankers at $22,300. Company owns 81 vessels as of March 31, 2025.
